title: LATAM Physician Credentialing & Privileging Gap
problem: Physician credentialing in LATAM is slow and manual. Hospitals take 60-90 days to credential new doctors. Paper-based verification of licenses, training, malpractice history. No shared credentialing database. Doctors credentialed separately at each hospital. Delays in starting work. No standardized privileging process.
solution: Physician credentialing platform. Centralized credential database. Automated license verification with medical councils. Shared credentialing across hospital network. Privileging workflow management. Expiration tracking and reminders. Integration with hospital HR systems.
technology: Document management, API integration with medical councils, workflow automation, shared database, expiration tracking
target_customer: Hospital systems, health insurers (network credentialing), medical staffing agencies, individual physicians
traction_signals: Credentialing delays costly for hospitals and doctors
regulatory_notes: Medical licensing regulations, credentialing standards vary by country
varden_fit: HIGH FIT - Credentialing is pure admin infrastructure. B2B software. Network effects (once hospitals share database). High switching costs. Boring but essential.
notes: BOREALING: Credentialing is the most boring admin process imaginable. But it's also incredibly time-consuming and expensive. Hospitals pay well to automate this.
